title: Debrief (machine-checkable extract)
description: The units/<UID>/debrief.json artifact (JSON-only; no debrief.md). Gates the evidence-table plumbing (claim types drawn from references/evidence-standards.md), the budget report, and the ANSWERED 4-key socratic block (result of the pre-output self-interrogation). The executor reasons free-form in its reply, then writes this JSON; the narrative lands in the `result`/`handoff_notes` string fields.

socratic.description: CANONICAL 4-key socratic block. Result of the free-form FORK.COUNTER.ADMIT.PIVOT.RESIDUAL self-interrogation, never the dialogue itself. counter must record an OUTCOME (validator rejects blank/'n/a'; mechanical-unit sentinel = 'unit is mechanical; no material premise to break'). confidence starts high|medium|low (same vocabulary as the top-level `confidence` enum; the socratic value may add a trailing '— <uncertainty>').

footprint.description: Actual token usage report. within_budget is defined RELATIVE TO THIS UNIT: within_budget := (tokens_consumed <= this unit's brief.budget_tokens), NOT the global 32K (F2). tokens_consumed has NO maximum (IMP-04/PR-6): a REAL overrun must be recordable truthfully rather than forced to lie under the old maximum:32000 cap. The plan-side 32K ceiling lives on brief.budget_tokens / graph.est_footprint_tokens (untouched — you still cannot PLAN >32K). validate_run.py I5 FAILs a tokens_consumed > brief.budget_tokens paired with within_budget:true. The if/then below is a coarser schema-only backstop (a schema cannot read the per-unit brief.budget_tokens): any report of tokens_consumed>32000 MUST ALSO self-identify as over-budget (within_budget:false), so the re-atomization discipline (SKILL.md Scope note) consumes an honest signal.

prior_feedback.description: Retries only (iteration>1): verbatim echo of iteration n-1's verify.feedback (actionable_changes + do_not_touch) plus >=1 concrete change made in response (AO-6). Home for what was formerly the debrief.md 'Prior feedback' section under JSON-only artifacts. On a retry the presence + non-empty changes_made + do_not_touch echo are now SCHEMA-REQUIRED (see the allOf clause below — IMP-05/PR-6); the echo's CONTENT stays executor-attested.

allOf.description: Retry echo mandatory (IMP-05/PR-6). A retry (iteration>=2) MUST carry the prior_feedback echo, and that echo MUST record >=1 concrete change (changes_made non-empty, AO-6) plus the do_not_touch echo field (present; may be [] for an honest 'nothing passed yet', AO-2). Closes the 'evasion by omission' half of Limitation F (I14/I15 were presence-gated: a retry omitting the whole block skipped both checks). A schema-invalid retry debrief is dropped before the I14/I15 comparison, so this is now the load-bearing presence gate; I14/I15 remain as the semantic backstop over schema-valid retries. First attempts (iteration:1) are untouched.
